test_cases.description: Verify that the oauth package defines the Provider interface with the four required methods and that a compliant struct satisfies the interface.
preconditions: internal/oauth package is compilable
A mock struct implementing all four methods (Name, AuthorizeURL, Exchange, UserInfo) is defined in the test
test_cases.input: A struct that implements Name() string, AuthorizeURL(state, redirectURI string) string, Exchange(ctx, code, redirectURI string) (string, error), UserInfo(ctx, accessToken string) (*UserInfo, error)
test_cases.expected: The struct can be assigned to a variable of type oauth.Provider without a compile error; all four methods are callable via the interface
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: var _ Provider = &mockProvider{} // compile-time assertion; assert no compilation error

test_cases.description: Verify that UserInfo struct is exported with the three required fields Username, Email, and ProviderID of type string.
preconditions: internal/oauth package is compiled
test_cases.input: Instantiate oauth.UserInfo with all fields set
test_cases.expected: UserInfo{Username: "user", Email: "user@example.com", ProviderID: "12345"} compiles and fields are accessible
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
ui := UserInfo{Username: "octocat", Email: "octocat@github.com", ProviderID: "1"}
assert ui.Username == "octocat"
assert ui.Email == "octocat@github.com"
assert ui.ProviderID == "1"

test_cases.description: Verify that the root apikit package re-exports Provider and UserInfo as type aliases interchangeable with oauth.Provider and oauth.UserInfo.
preconditions: root apikit package compiles and imports internal/oauth
test_cases.input: A value of type oauth.Provider assigned to apikit.Provider variable and vice versa
test_cases.expected: Both assignments compile without error; values are interchangeable
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
var p apikit.Provider = &mockProvider{} // compile-time check
var q oauth.Provider = p // also valid
assert p == q

test_cases.description: Verify that NewRegistry returns a non-nil empty Registry with no providers.
preconditions: internal/oauth package compiled
test_cases.expected: Returns non-nil *Registry; List() returns empty slice []string{}
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
r := NewRegistry()
assert r != nil
assert len(r.List()) == 0

test_cases.description: Verify that Register adds a provider and subsequent Get by the same name returns that provider.
preconditions: Registry created via NewRegistry()
A mock provider with Name() returning "testprovider" is available
test_cases.expected: Register returns nil error; Get("testprovider") returns the same provider instance
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
r := NewRegistry()
p := &mockProvider{name: "testprovider"}
err := r.Register(p)
assert err == nil
assert r.Get("testprovider") == p

test_cases.description: Verify that Get returns the registered provider for a known name and nil for an unknown name.
preconditions: Registry with one provider named "github" registered
test_cases.expected: Get("github") returns the provider; Get("unknown") returns nil
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
r := NewRegistry()
r.Register(&mockProvider{name: "github"})
assert r.Get("github") != nil
assert r.Get("unknown") == nil

test_cases.description: Verify that List returns all provider names in alphabetical order.
preconditions: Registry with providers "zebra", "alpha", and "mango" registered in that order
test_cases.expected: Returns ["alpha", "mango", "zebra"]
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
r := NewRegistry()
r.Register(&mockProvider{name: "zebra"})
r.Register(&mockProvider{name: "alpha"})
r.Register(&mockProvider{name: "mango"})
names := r.List()
assert names == ["alpha", "mango", "zebra"]

test_cases.description: Verify GitHub provider implements Provider interface, Name() returns 'github', and default URLs are used when config overrides are empty.
preconditions: GitHub provider constructed with clientID="cid", clientSecret="secret", empty URL overrides, and shared http.Client
test_cases.input: Call Name() on the GitHub provider
test_cases.expected: Name() returns "github"; provider satisfies oauth.Provider interface at compile time
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
var _ Provider = &GitHubProvider{} // compile-time
p := NewGitHubProvider("cid", "secret", "", "", "", httpClient)
assert p.Name() == "github"

test_cases.description: Verify AuthorizeURL constructs a correct URL with client_id, scope=user:email, state, and redirect_uri query parameters.
preconditions: GitHub provider constructed with clientID="myclient", default authorize_url
test_cases.expected: Returned URL has base https://github.com/login/oauth/authorize with query params client_id=myclient, scope=user:email, state=mystate, redirect_uri=http://localhost:9000/cb
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
p := NewGitHubProvider("myclient", "secret", "", "", "", httpClient)
url := p.AuthorizeURL("mystate", "http://localhost:9000/cb")
parsed := parseURL(url)
assert parsed.host == "github.com"
assert parsed.path == "/login/oauth/authorize"
assert parsed.query["client_id"] == "myclient"
assert parsed.query["scope"] == "user:email"
assert parsed.query["state"] == "mystate"
assert parsed.query["redirect_uri"] == "http://localhost:9000/cb"

test_cases.description: Verify Exchange sends correct POST request to token_url with required fields and Accept header, and returns access_token from JSON response.
preconditions: httptest.Server configured to respond with {"access_token": "tok123"} on POST to /token
GitHub provider constructed with token_url pointing to httptest.Server
test_cases.expected: POST sent to token_url with form body containing client_id, client_secret, code, redirect_uri; Accept: application/json header present; returns ("tok123", nil)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
server := httptest.NewServer(handler that captures request and responds with {"access_token":"tok123"})
p := NewGitHubProvider("cid", "csec", "", server.URL+"/token", "", httpClient)
tok, err := p.Exchange(ctx, "authcode", "http://localhost:9000/cb")
assert err == nil
assert tok == "tok123"
assert capturedRequest.method == "POST"
assert capturedRequest.header["Accept"] == "application/json"
assert capturedRequest.formBody["client_id"] == "cid"
assert capturedRequest.formBody["code"] == "authcode"
assert capturedRequest.formBody["redirect_uri"] == "http://localhost:9000/cb"

test_cases.description: Verify UserInfo sends GET with Bearer auth header and parses login, email, and id fields into UserInfo struct.
preconditions: httptest.Server configured to return {"login":"octocat","email":"cat@github.com","id":42} on GET /user
GitHub provider with userinfo_url pointing to httptest.Server
test_cases.expected: GET request to userinfo_url with Authorization: Bearer mytoken; returns *UserInfo{Username: "octocat", Email: "cat@github.com", ProviderID: "42"}, nil
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
server := httptest.NewServer(handler returning {"login":"octocat","email":"cat@github.com","id":42})
p := NewGitHubProvider("cid", "csec", "", "", server.URL+"/user", httpClient)
ui, err := p.UserInfo(ctx, "mytoken")
assert err == nil
assert ui.Username == "octocat"
assert ui.Email == "cat@github.com"
assert ui.ProviderID == "42"
assert capturedRequest.header["Authorization"] == "Bearer mytoken"

test_cases.description: Verify GitHub provider stores the injected http.Client and does not create its own client.
preconditions: A specific *http.Client instance created for injection
test_cases.expected: The provider struct's internal client field equals the injected customClient pointer

test_cases.description: Verify Config struct correctly unmarshals [[oauth.providers]] TOML into OAuthConfig.Providers slice.
preconditions: TOML string with [[oauth.providers]] entry containing name, client_id, client_secret, and optional URL overrides
test_cases.expected: Config.OAuth.Providers[0].Name == "github", ClientID == "cid", ClientSecret == "csec", AuthorizeURL == "https://custom.example.com/auth"
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
cfg := loadConfigFromString(tomlString)
assert cfg.OAuth.Providers[0].Name == "github"
assert cfg.OAuth.Providers[0].ClientID == "cid"
assert cfg.OAuth.Providers[0].ClientSecret == "csec"
assert cfg.OAuth.Providers[0].AuthorizeURL == "https://custom.example.com/auth"

test_cases.description: Verify LoadConfig returns (nil, error) when a provider entry is missing client_id.
preconditions: TOML config with [[oauth.providers]] entry that has name and client_secret but no client_id
test_cases.input: TOML with provider entry missing client_id
test_cases.expected: LoadConfig returns (nil, non-nil error) describing the missing client_id field
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
cfg, err := LoadConfig(tomlMissingClientID)
assert cfg == nil
assert err != nil
assert errorContains(err, "client_id")

test_cases.description: Verify LoadConfig returns (nil, error) when two provider entries share the same name.
preconditions: TOML config with two [[oauth.providers]] entries both having name="github"
test_cases.expected: LoadConfig returns (nil, non-nil error) indicating duplicate provider name
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
cfg, err := LoadConfig(tomlDuplicateNames)
assert cfg == nil
assert err != nil
assert errorContains(err, "duplicate") or errorContains(err, "github")

test_cases.description: Verify server bootstrap creates GitHub provider from config and registers it; returns startup error for unknown provider names.
preconditions: Valid config with one github provider entry
NewRegistry available
test_cases.expected: Registry contains provider named "github"; Get("github") is non-nil; for name "gitlab" bootstrap returns non-nil error
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
registry, err := BuildRegistryFromConfig(config, httpClient)
assert err == nil
assert registry.Get("github") != nil

config2 := configWithProvider("gitlab")
_, err2 := BuildRegistryFromConfig(config2, httpClient)
assert err2 != nil

test_cases.description: Verify LoadConfig accepts empty [[oauth.providers]] array as valid and registry has no providers.
preconditions: TOML config with empty or absent [[oauth.providers]] section
test_cases.expected: LoadConfig returns (*Config, nil); resulting registry List() returns empty slice
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
cfg, err := LoadConfig(tomlNoProviders)
assert err == nil
assert cfg != nil
assert len(cfg.OAuth.Providers) == 0
registry := NewRegistry()
assert len(registry.List()) == 0

test_cases.description: Verify GET /auth/providers returns HTTP 200 with JSON array containing name and authorize_url for each registered provider.
preconditions: Echo server started with RegisterOAuthHandlers called
Registry has one github provider registered with clientID="cid"
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200; Content-Type: application/json; body is [{"name":"github","authorize_url":"https://github.com/login/oauth/authorize?client_id=cid&scope=user%3Aemail"}]
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp := httpGet("/auth/providers")
assert resp.statusCode == 200
assert resp.contentType == "application/json"
body := parseJSON(resp.body)
assert len(body) == 1
assert body[0]["name"] == "github"
assert contains(body[0]["authorize_url"], "client_id=cid")
assert contains(body[0]["authorize_url"], "scope=user%3Aemail")

test_cases.description: Verify GET /auth/providers returns HTTP 200 with empty JSON array when no providers are registered.
preconditions: Echo server started with RegisterOAuthHandlers called
Registry is empty (no providers registered)
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200; body is []
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp := httpGet("/auth/providers")
assert resp.statusCode == 200
body := parseJSON(resp.body)
assert body is empty array

test_cases.description: Verify GET /auth/providers response includes Cache-Control: public, max-age=300 header.
preconditions: Echo server started with CacheMiddleware(CachePublic) applied to GET /auth/providers route
test_cases.expected: Response header Cache-Control equals "public, max-age=300"
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp := httpGet("/auth/providers")
assert resp.headers["Cache-Control"] == "public, max-age=300"

test_cases.description: Verify GET /auth/providers response does not include client_secret, token_url, or userinfo_url fields.
preconditions: Echo server started with github provider registered with clientSecret="supersecret", token_url, and userinfo_url configured
test_cases.expected: Response body JSON objects contain only 'name' and 'authorize_url' fields; no client_secret, token_url, or userinfo_url
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp := httpGet("/auth/providers")
body := parseJSON(resp.body)
for each entry in body:
  assert keys(entry) == ["name", "authorize_url"]
  assert "client_secret" not in entry
  assert "token_url" not in entry
  assert "userinfo_url" not in entry

test_cases.description: Verify callback handler proceeds without validation error when provider, code, and redirect_uri are all non-empty and expires is a valid value.
preconditions: Echo server with handler mounted
Registry has "github" provider registered
Mock provider Exchange returns success
test_cases.expected: No HTTP 400 returned from validation phase; handler proceeds to provider lookup
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp := httpPost("/auth/callback", {provider:"github", code:"abc", redirect_uri:"http://localhost:9000/cb", expires:30})
assert resp.statusCode != 400 or resp body does not contain validation error messages

test_cases.description: Verify callback handler returns HTTP 400 with message 'provider is required' when provider field is missing.
preconditions: Echo server with POST /auth/callback handler mounted
test_cases.expected: HTTP 400; body {"error":{"code":400,"message":"provider is required"}}
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp := httpPost("/auth/callback", {code:"abc", redirect_uri:"http://localhost:9000/cb"})
assert resp.statusCode == 400
body := parseJSON(resp.body)
assert body["error"]["code"] == 400
assert body["error"]["message"] == "provider is required"

test_cases.description: Verify callback handler returns HTTP 400 with message 'code is required' when code field is missing.
preconditions: Echo server with POST /auth/callback handler mounted
test_cases.expected: HTTP 400; body {"error":{"code":400,"message":"code is required"}}
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp := httpPost("/auth/callback", {provider:"github", redirect_uri:"http://localhost:9000/cb"})
assert resp.statusCode == 400
body := parseJSON(resp.body)
assert body["error"]["message"] == "code is required"

test_cases.description: Verify callback handler returns HTTP 400 with message 'redirect_uri is required' when redirect_uri field is missing.
preconditions: Echo server with POST /auth/callback handler mounted
test_cases.expected: HTTP 400; body {"error":{"code":400,"message":"redirect_uri is required"}}
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp := httpPost("/auth/callback", {provider:"github", code:"abc"})
assert resp.statusCode == 400
body := parseJSON(resp.body)
assert body["error"]["message"] == "redirect_uri is required"

test_cases.description: Verify callback handler returns HTTP 400 with message 'expires must be 0, 30, 60, or 90' when expires has an invalid value.
preconditions: Echo server with POST /auth/callback handler mounted
test_cases.expected: HTTP 400; body {"error":{"code":400,"message":"expires must be 0, 30, 60, or 90"}}
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp := httpPost("/auth/callback", {provider:"github", code:"abc", redirect_uri:"http://localhost:9000/cb", expires:45})
assert resp.statusCode == 400
body := parseJSON(resp.body)
assert body["error"]["message"] == "expires must be 0, 30, 60, or 90"

test_cases.description: Verify callback handler defaults expires to 90 when the expires field is absent from the request body.
preconditions: Echo server with POST /auth/callback handler mounted
Mock provider and mock DB that capture the expires value used in key generation
test_cases.expected: Handler internally uses expires=90; the resulting api_key.expires_at is set to now+90 days
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp := httpPost("/auth/callback", {provider:"github", code:"abc", redirect_uri:"http://localhost:9000/cb"})
// If full flow completes:
if resp.statusCode == 200:
  body := parseJSON(resp.body)
  expiresAt := parseRFC3339(body["api_key"]["expires_at"])
  diff := expiresAt - now()
  assert diff >= 89*24h and diff <= 91*24h

test_cases.description: Verify redirect URI validator accepts http://localhost:<port>/path for various valid port numbers.
preconditions: Redirect URI validator function available
test_cases.expected: All four URIs are accepted (no error returned)

test_cases.description: Verify redirect URI validator accepts URIs whose scheme and host match external_url when external_url is configured.
preconditions: external_url configured as "https://api.example.com"
test_cases.expected: Both URIs accepted (no error)

test_cases.description: Verify redirect URI validator accepts only localhost URIs when external_url is empty, rejecting non-localhost URIs.
preconditions: external_url is empty string
test_cases.expected: Localhost URI returns nil error; non-localhost URI returns non-nil error
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
err1 := ValidateRedirectURI("http://localhost:9000/cb", externalURL="")
assert err1 == nil
err2 := ValidateRedirectURI("https://example.com/cb", externalURL="")
assert err2 != nil

test_cases.description: Verify POST /auth/callback returns HTTP 400 with 'redirect_uri is not allowed' when redirect_uri fails allowlist validation.
preconditions: Echo server with POST /auth/callback handler mounted
Registry has "github" provider
external_url not configured (empty)
test_cases.expected: HTTP 400; body {"error":{"code":400,"message":"redirect_uri is not allowed"}}; provider.Exchange is never called
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp := httpPost("/auth/callback", {provider:"github", code:"abc", redirect_uri:"https://evil.example.com/steal"})
assert resp.statusCode == 400
body := parseJSON(resp.body)
assert body["error"]["message"] == "redirect_uri is not allowed"
assert exchangeCallCount == 0 // Exchange was never invoked

test_cases.description: Verify callback handler calls provider.Exchange after successful provider lookup and redirect URI validation.
preconditions: Echo server with handler mounted
Registry has "github" provider (mock that tracks Exchange calls)
redirect_uri is valid localhost URI
test_cases.expected: provider.Exchange is called with (ctx, "mycode", "http://localhost:9000/cb"); returns non-empty access token
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
exchangeCalled := false
mockProvider.Exchange = func(ctx, code, redirectURI) {
  exchangeCalled = true
  assert code == "mycode"
  assert redirectURI == "http://localhost:9000/cb"
  return "token123", nil
}
httpPost("/auth/callback", {provider:"github", code:"mycode", redirect_uri:"http://localhost:9000/cb"})
assert exchangeCalled == true

test_cases.description: Verify callback handler returns HTTP 401 with 'authorization code exchange failed' when Exchange returns an error.
preconditions: Echo server with handler mounted
Mock provider Exchange returns ("" , error("bad code"))
test_cases.input: POST /auth/callback with valid body; mock Exchange returns error
test_cases.expected: HTTP 401; body {"error":{"code":401,"message":"authorization code exchange failed"}}
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
mockProvider.Exchange = func() { return "", errors.New("bad code") }
resp := httpPost("/auth/callback", validBody)
assert resp.statusCode == 401
body := parseJSON(resp.body)
assert body["error"]["message"] == "authorization code exchange failed"

test_cases.description: Verify callback handler calls provider.UserInfo with the access token after successful Exchange.
preconditions: Mock provider Exchange returns "tok123"
Mock provider UserInfo tracks calls and returns valid *UserInfo
test_cases.expected: UserInfo is called with (ctx, "tok123"); returns non-nil *UserInfo with Username and ProviderID populated
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
userInfoCalled := false
mockProvider.UserInfo = func(ctx, accessToken) {
  userInfoCalled = true
  assert accessToken == "tok123"
  return &UserInfo{Username:"octocat", Email:"cat@github.com", ProviderID:"1"}, nil
}
httpPost("/auth/callback", validBody)
assert userInfoCalled == true

test_cases.description: Verify callback handler returns HTTP 502 with correct message when UserInfo returns an error.
preconditions: Mock provider Exchange returns success
Mock provider UserInfo returns (nil, error("upstream failed"))
test_cases.expected: HTTP 502; body {"error":{"code":502,"message":"failed to retrieve user info from provider"}}
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
mockProvider.UserInfo = func() { return nil, errors.New("upstream failed") }
resp := httpPost("/auth/callback", validBody)
assert resp.statusCode == 502
body := parseJSON(resp.body)
assert body["error"]["message"] == "failed to retrieve user info from provider"

test_cases.description: Verify callback handler returns HTTP 400 with 'provider returned empty email; email is required' when UserInfo returns empty Email.
preconditions: Mock provider Exchange returns success
Mock provider UserInfo returns &UserInfo{Username:"octocat", Email:"", ProviderID:"1"}
test_cases.expected: HTTP 400; body {"error":{"code":400,"message":"provider returned empty email; email is required"}}
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
mockProvider.UserInfo = func() { return &UserInfo{Username:"octocat", Email:"", ProviderID:"1"}, nil }
resp := httpPost("/auth/callback", validBody)
assert resp.statusCode == 400
body := parseJSON(resp.body)
assert body["error"]["message"] == "provider returned empty email; email is required"

test_cases.description: Verify callback handler opens a database transaction and queries users table by (provider, provider_id) after successful user info retrieval.
preconditions: SQLite database initialised with users and api_keys tables
Mock provider returns valid UserInfo with ProviderID="99"
No user with (provider="github", provider_id="99") exists in users table
test_cases.input: POST /auth/callback with valid body for github provider
test_cases.expected: db.WithTx is called; SELECT from users WHERE provider='github' AND provider_id='99' executes without error

test_cases.description: Verify a new user is inserted with correct fields including admin role when email matches admin_email and no admin exists.
preconditions: SQLite database with admin_config row setting admin_email='admin@example.com'
No user with role='admin' exists in users table
Mock provider returns UserInfo{Username:"newadmin", Email:"admin@example.com", ProviderID:"55"}
test_cases.input: POST /auth/callback for github provider with code for admin@example.com
test_cases.expected: New user row in users table with role='admin', status='active', provider='github', provider_id='55'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp := httpPost("/auth/callback", validBody)
assert resp.statusCode == 200
user := db.QueryRow("SELECT role, status FROM users WHERE provider_id='55'")
assert user.role == "admin"
assert user.status == "active"

test_cases.description: Verify an existing active user's username, email, and updated_at are updated on re-login, while other fields remain unchanged.
preconditions: SQLite database with existing user row: provider='github', provider_id='77', username='oldname', email='old@example.com', role='user', status='active'
test_cases.input: POST /auth/callback with mock UserInfo{Username:"newname", Email:"new@example.com", ProviderID:"77"}
test_cases.expected: users row for provider_id='77' has username='newname', email='new@example.com', updated_at changed; id, role, status, created_at unchanged
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
originalID := db.QueryRow("SELECT id FROM users WHERE provider_id='77'").id
originalCreatedAt := db.QueryRow("SELECT created_at FROM users WHERE provider_id='77'").created_at
httpPost("/auth/callback", validBody)
user := db.QueryRow("SELECT * FROM users WHERE provider_id='77'")
assert user.username == "newname"
assert user.email == "new@example.com"
assert user.id == originalID
assert user.created_at == originalCreatedAt
assert user.status == "active"

test_cases.description: Verify callback handler returns HTTP 403 and does not update user or create key when existing user is blocked.
preconditions: SQLite database with user row: provider='github', provider_id='88', status='blocked'
Mock provider returns UserInfo{ProviderID:"88", Email:"blocked@example.com"}
test_cases.expected: HTTP 403; body {"error":{"code":403,"message":"user is blocked"}}; users row unchanged; no new row in api_keys for this user
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
keyCountBefore := db.QueryRow("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM api_keys WHERE user_id=(SELECT id FROM users WHERE provider_id='88')").count
resp := httpPost("/auth/callback", validBody)
assert resp.statusCode == 403
body := parseJSON(resp.body)
assert body["error"]["message"] == "user is blocked"
keyCountAfter := db.QueryRow("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM api_keys WHERE user_id=(SELECT id FROM users WHERE provider_id='88')").count
assert keyCountAfter == keyCountBefore
user := db.QueryRow("SELECT status FROM users WHERE provider_id='88'")
assert user.status == "blocked"

test_cases.description: Verify that all previously active API keys for the user are revoked (revoked_at set) when a new key is generated on login.
preconditions: SQLite database with existing user and one active api_key row (revoked_at IS NULL) for that user
test_cases.input: POST /auth/callback for the user who already has an active key
test_cases.expected: Old api_key row has revoked_at set to non-null timestamp; new api_key row created with revoked_at NULL
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
oldKeyID := db.QueryRow("SELECT key_id FROM api_keys WHERE user_id=? AND revoked_at IS NULL", userID).key_id
httpPost("/auth/callback", validBody)
oldKey := db.QueryRow("SELECT revoked_at FROM api_keys WHERE key_id=?", oldKeyID)
assert oldKey.revoked_at IS NOT NULL
newActiveKeys := db.Query("SELECT * FROM api_keys WHERE user_id=? AND revoked_at IS NULL", userID)
assert len(newActiveKeys) == 1

test_cases.description: Verify new API key is generated with correct key_id length, secret length, SHA-256 hash stored, and expires_at calculated correctly.
preconditions: SQLite database initialised
Mock provider returns valid UserInfo
Request body has expires=30
test_cases.expected: api_keys row has key_id of length 8, secret_hash = SHA256(plaintext_secret), expires_at approximately now+30days; response api_key.key matches format ak_<8chars>_<32chars>
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp := httpPost("/auth/callback", {provider:"github", code:"abc", redirect_uri:"http://localhost:9000/cb", expires:30})
assert resp.statusCode == 200
body := parseJSON(resp.body)
keyStr := body["api_key"]["key"]
parts := split(keyStr, "_")
assert len(parts) == 3
assert parts[0] == "ak"
assert len(parts[1]) == 8
assert len(parts[2]) == 32
expectedHash := sha256(parts[2])
dbKey := db.QueryRow("SELECT secret_hash, expires_at, key_id FROM api_keys WHERE revoked_at IS NULL")
assert dbKey.secret_hash == hex(expectedHash)
assert dbKey.key_id == parts[1]
diff := parseTime(dbKey.expires_at) - now()
assert diff >= 29*24h and diff <= 31*24h

test_cases.description: Verify key generation function uses crypto/rand (not math/rand) for both key_id and secret generation.
preconditions: Key generation function is implemented in internal/oauth
Code review or static analysis available
test_cases.expected: crypto/rand is imported and used; math/rand is not imported in key generation code path

test_cases.description: Verify callback response api_key.key follows format <TokenPrefix>_<key_id>_<secret> and plaintext secret is not stored in the database.
preconditions: SQLite database initialised
Mock provider returns valid UserInfo
test_cases.expected: Response api_key.key matches "ak_<8alnum>_<32alnum>"; api_keys.secret_hash != plaintext secret; SHA256(plaintext)==secret_hash
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp := httpPost("/auth/callback", validBody)
body := parseJSON(resp.body)
key := body["api_key"]["key"]
assert matchesRegex(key, "^ak_[a-zA-Z0-9]{8}_[a-zA-Z0-9]{32}$")
parts := split(key, "_")
plainSecret := parts[2]
dbRow := db.QueryRow("SELECT secret_hash FROM api_keys WHERE revoked_at IS NULL ORDER BY rowid DESC LIMIT 1")
assert dbRow.secret_hash != plainSecret
assert dbRow.secret_hash == hex(sha256(plainSecret))

test_cases.description: Verify callback returns HTTP 200 with correct user object and api_key object structure on full successful flow.
preconditions: SQLite database initialised
Mock GitHub provider httptest.Server returns valid token and user info
Registry has github provider
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200; body has user.id (UUID), user.username, user.email, user.full_name, user.status='active', user.role, user.provider='github', user.provider_id, user.created_at (RFC3339), user.updated_at (RFC3339), api_key.key (ak_<8>_<32>), api_key.key_id (8 chars), api_key.expires_at (RFC3339 ~90 days out)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp := httpPost("/auth/callback", validBody)
assert resp.statusCode == 200
body := parseJSON(resp.body)
user := body["user"]
assert isUUID(user["id"])
assert user["status"] == "active"
assert user["provider"] == "github"
assert isRFC3339(user["created_at"])
assert isRFC3339(user["updated_at"])
apiKey := body["api_key"]
assert matchesRegex(apiKey["key"], "^ak_[a-zA-Z0-9]{8}_[a-zA-Z0-9]{32}$")
assert len(apiKey["key_id"]) == 8
assert isRFC3339(apiKey["expires_at"])

test_cases.description: Verify the callback response user object includes the role field set to either 'admin' or 'user'.
preconditions: SQLite database initialised
Mock provider returns valid UserInfo with a non-admin email
test_cases.input: POST /auth/callback for a new regular user
test_cases.expected: Response body user.role is "user"
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp := httpPost("/auth/callback", validBody)
body := parseJSON(resp.body)
assert body["user"]["role"] == "user" or body["user"]["role"] == "admin"
// For non-admin email:
assert body["user"]["role"] == "user"

test_cases.description: Verify all timestamps in the response are valid RFC 3339 strings and api_key.expires_at is null when expires=0.
preconditions: SQLite database initialised
Mock provider returns valid UserInfo
test_cases.expected: user.created_at and user.updated_at are valid RFC 3339 strings; api_key.expires_at is JSON null
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp := httpPost("/auth/callback", {provider:"github", code:"abc", redirect_uri:"http://localhost:9000/cb", expires:0})
body := parseJSON(resp.body)
assert isRFC3339(body["user"]["created_at"])
assert isRFC3339(body["user"]["updated_at"])
assert body["api_key"]["expires_at"] == null

test_cases.description: Verify RegisterOAuthHandlers mounts GET /auth/providers with CachePublic middleware and POST /auth/callback on the Echo group.
preconditions: Echo server with APIGroup() configured
RegisterOAuthHandlers called with registry, db, externalURL
test_cases.expected: GET /auth/providers returns 200 with Cache-Control: public, max-age=300; POST /auth/callback returns 400 (validation) not 404
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
RegisterOAuthHandlers(group, registry, db, "")
resp1 := httpGet("/auth/providers")
assert resp1.statusCode == 200
assert resp1.headers["Cache-Control"] == "public, max-age=300"
resp2 := httpPost("/auth/callback", {})
assert resp2.statusCode == 400 // validation error, not 404

test_cases.description: Verify server bootstrap creates a single shared http.Client with 30-second timeout using DefaultTransport and injects it into all providers.
preconditions: Server bootstrap function available
test_cases.input: Bootstrap call that creates github provider
test_cases.expected: The http.Client has Timeout=30s; the provider's internal httpClient pointer equals the shared client
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
client, registry := bootstrapOAuthProviders(config)
assert client.Timeout == 30 * time.Second
assert client.Transport == http.DefaultTransport
githubProvider := registry.Get("github")
assert githubProvider.httpClient == client

test_cases.description: Verify concurrent POST /auth/callback requests for the same user are handled without data corruption, relying on SQLite single-connection serialization.
preconditions: SQLite database with SetMaxOpenConns(1) as configured by database_layer
Mock provider returns valid UserInfo for ProviderID="concurrent-user"
test_cases.input: Two goroutines simultaneously POST /auth/callback for the same user (provider_id="concurrent-user")
test_cases.expected: Both requests complete without deadlock or error; no data corruption in users or api_keys tables

test_cases.description: Verify that after two sequential callback transactions for the same user, exactly one active key remains in api_keys.
preconditions: SQLite database initialised
Mock provider returns consistent UserInfo for ProviderID="serial-user"
test_cases.input: Two sequential POST /auth/callback requests for the same user
test_cases.expected: After both requests, exactly one api_keys row with revoked_at IS NULL exists for the user; previous keys are revoked
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
httpPost("/auth/callback", validBody) // first login
httpPost("/auth/callback", validBody) // second login
activeKeys := db.Query("SELECT * FROM api_keys WHERE user_id=? AND revoked_at IS NULL", userID)
assert len(activeKeys) == 1
revokedKeys := db.Query("SELECT * FROM api_keys WHERE user_id=? AND revoked_at IS NOT NULL", userID)
assert len(revokedKeys) == 1

test_cases.description: Verify removing a provider from config does not delete existing user records or api_keys, and credentials remain valid.
preconditions: SQLite database with user row where provider='github'
Active api_key for that user
Server restarted with [[oauth.providers]] section empty
test_cases.input: Check users and api_keys tables after server restart with empty provider config
test_cases.expected: Users row still exists with original provider and provider_id; api_keys row still exists and is active
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
// Before restart:
originalUser := db.QueryRow("SELECT * FROM users WHERE provider='github'")
originalKey := db.QueryRow("SELECT * FROM api_keys WHERE revoked_at IS NULL")
// Restart server with empty providers config
restartServerWithConfig(emptyProvidersConfig)
// After restart:
userAfter := db.QueryRow("SELECT * FROM users WHERE provider='github'")
assert userAfter != nil
assert userAfter.provider_id == originalUser.provider_id
keyAfter := db.QueryRow("SELECT * FROM api_keys WHERE key_id=?", originalKey.key_id)
assert keyAfter != nil
assert keyAfter.revoked_at IS NULL

test_cases.description: Verify POST /auth/callback returns HTTP 400 with 'unknown provider: github' after github is removed from config.
preconditions: Server running with empty providers registry (github removed from config)
test_cases.expected: HTTP 400; body {"error":{"code":400,"message":"unknown provider: github"}}
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp := httpPost("/auth/callback", {provider:"github", code:"abc", redirect_uri:"http://localhost:9000/cb"})
assert resp.statusCode == 400
body := parseJSON(resp.body)
assert body["error"]["message"] == "unknown provider: github"

property_tests.description: For any successful POST /auth/callback response, exactly one active (non-revoked, non-expired) key exists for the user in api_keys after the transaction commits.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate random valid OAuth callback requests for the same user repeated N times (N between 1 and 10); each request uses a fresh mock provider returning the same ProviderID
property_tests.invariant_check: After each successful callback response, query api_keys WHERE user_id=<id> AND revoked_at IS NULL; assert count == 1. Count of revoked keys must equal (N-1) after N logins.

property_tests.description: For any POST /auth/callback request for a blocked user, the user status remains 'blocked', no new key is created, and HTTP 403 is returned.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate any combination of valid provider, code, and redirect_uri for a user whose status='blocked' in the database; run M iterations (M between 1 and 5)
property_tests.invariant_check: After each request: resp.statusCode == 403; db.users WHERE provider_id=<id> has status='blocked'; db.api_keys WHERE user_id=<id> count is unchanged from before request

property_tests.description: For any new API key inserted into api_keys, the plaintext secret is never written to the database; only the SHA-256 hash is stored.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate N successful callback responses (N between 1 and 20); collect the plaintext secret from each response body and the secret_hash from api_keys
property_tests.invariant_check: For each (plaintext_secret, secret_hash) pair: secret_hash == hex(sha256(plaintext_secret)); api_keys.secret_hash != plaintext_secret; no other column in api_keys contains plaintext_secret

property_tests.description: Admin auto-grant fires at most once across any number of OAuth callback requests from users whose email matches admin_email.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate K callback requests (K between 2 and 10) from different provider_ids but all with email matching admin_email; vary order of requests
property_tests.invariant_check: After all K requests: db.users WHERE role='admin' count == 1; only the first user to create when no admin existed gets role='admin'; all subsequent users with matching email get role='user'

property_tests.description: No two providers with the same name can coexist in the Registry for any sequence of Register() calls.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate random sequences of Register() calls with provider names chosen from a small alphabet (e.g. 'a', 'b', 'c'); include duplicate names in the sequence
property_tests.invariant_check: After each sequence: for any name n, exactly 0 or 1 provider exists under n; duplicate Register() returns non-nil error; total registered providers == count of unique names in the sequence

property_tests.description: For any GET /auth/providers response, the body contains only 'name' and 'authorize_url' fields per entry, never secrets.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Register N providers (N between 0 and 5) with random client_ids and client_secrets; call GET /auth/providers
property_tests.invariant_check: For each JSON object in the response array: keys(object) == {"name", "authorize_url"}; 'client_secret' not present; 'token_url' not present; 'userinfo_url' not present

property_tests.description: provider.Exchange() is never called when redirect_uri fails allowlist validation, for any disallowed redirect_uri input.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate random redirect_uri strings that do not match localhost pattern or external_url (e.g. ftp://, https://attacker.com, file:///etc/passwd, empty string); set external_url to 'https://api.example.com'
property_tests.invariant_check: For each invalid redirect_uri: resp.statusCode == 400; exchangeCallCount == 0 (Exchange was never invoked); resp body error message == 'redirect_uri is not allowed'

property_tests.description: User upsert and key creation are atomic: if any database step fails, no partial user updates or partial key records are visible.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Inject faults at random points in the db.WithTx callback (e.g. fail after user insert but before key insert, fail after revoke but before new key insert); attempt N requests with injected faults
property_tests.invariant_check: After each fault-injected request: response is HTTP 500; db.users count for the new provider_id == 0 (if insert was first step that failed) or unchanged from before; db.api_keys count for the user unchanged from before the request; no partial rows visible

edge_case_tests.description: Verify compile-time rejection when a type implements only a subset of the Provider interface methods.
preconditions: Test file with a struct that implements only Name() and AuthorizeURL() but not Exchange() or UserInfo()
edge_case_tests.input: var _ oauth.Provider = &incompleteProvider{} in a test file
edge_case_tests.expected: Compilation fails with a missing-method error listing Exchange and UserInfo

edge_case_tests.description: Verify Registry.Register returns a non-nil error for a duplicate provider name without replacing the existing provider.
preconditions: Registry with provider named "github" already registered
edge_case_tests.expected: Returns non-nil error; registry still returns original provider from Get("github")
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
r := NewRegistry()
original := &mockProvider{name: "github", id: 1}
duplicate := &mockProvider{name: "github", id: 2}
r.Register(original)
err := r.Register(duplicate)
assert err != nil
assert r.Get("github") == original // original not replaced

edge_case_tests.description: Verify Registry.Get returns nil when called with an empty string.
preconditions: Registry with one provider named "github" registered
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
r := NewRegistry()
r.Register(&mockProvider{name: "github"})
result := r.Get("")
assert result == nil

edge_case_tests.description: Verify GitHub provider Exchange returns an error when token endpoint responds with an error field in JSON.
preconditions: httptest.Server configured to return {"error":"bad_verification_code","error_description":"The code passed is incorrect"}
edge_case_tests.expected: Returns ("", non-nil error); error message contains the provider error value
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
server := httptest.NewServer(handler returning {"error":"bad_verification_code","error_description":"The code passed is incorrect"})
p := NewGitHubProvider("cid", "csec", "", server.URL, "", httpClient)
tok, err := p.Exchange(ctx, "badcode", "http://localhost:9000/cb")
assert tok == ""
assert err != nil
assert contains(err.Error(), "bad_verification_code")

edge_case_tests.description: Verify GitHub provider Exchange returns an error when the token endpoint response has an empty or absent access_token.
preconditions: httptest.Server configured to return {"token_type":"bearer"} (no access_token field)
edge_case_tests.expected: Returns ("", non-nil error) indicating token was not returned
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
server := httptest.NewServer(handler returning {"token_type":"bearer"})
p := NewGitHubProvider("cid", "csec", "", server.URL, "", httpClient)
tok, err := p.Exchange(ctx, "code", "http://localhost:9000/cb")
assert tok == ""
assert err != nil

edge_case_tests.description: Verify GitHub provider Exchange and UserInfo return errors without retry when HTTP call times out or returns non-2xx status.
preconditions: httptest.Server that returns HTTP 500 for token endpoint
GitHub provider with token_url pointing to that server
edge_case_tests.expected: Returns ("", non-nil error) wrapping the HTTP error; no retry attempt
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
server := httptest.NewServer(handler returning HTTP 500)
p := NewGitHubProvider("cid", "csec", "", server.URL, "", httpClient)
start := now()
tok, err := p.Exchange(ctx, "code", "http://localhost:9000/cb")
end := now()
assert tok == ""
assert err != nil
assert (end - start) < 2 * time.Second // no retry delay

edge_case_tests.description: Verify GitHub provider Exchange and UserInfo propagate context cancellation without hanging.
preconditions: httptest.Server that delays response by 5 seconds
Context with 100ms deadline
edge_case_tests.expected: Returns error within ~100ms; error wraps context.DeadlineExceeded or context.Canceled; no goroutine leak
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
ctx, cancel := contextWithTimeout(100 * time.Millisecond)
defer cancel()
server := httptest.NewServer(handler that sleeps 5 seconds)
p := NewGitHubProvider("cid", "csec", "", server.URL, "", httpClient)
start := now()
tok, err := p.Exchange(ctx, "code", "http://localhost:9000/cb")
end := now()
assert tok == "" or tok == nil
assert err != nil
assert (end - start) < 500 * time.Millisecond

edge_case_tests.description: Verify server bootstrap returns a startup error when config contains a provider with unknown name (e.g. 'gitlab').
preconditions: Config with [[oauth.providers]] entry name='gitlab', client_id='id', client_secret='secret'
edge_case_tests.expected: Returns non-nil error naming the unsupported provider 'gitlab'; no partial registry is exposed; server does not start
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
config := Config{OAuth: {Providers: [{Name:"gitlab", ClientID:"id", ClientSecret:"sec"}]}}
registry, err := BuildRegistryFromConfig(config, httpClient)
assert err != nil
assert contains(err.Error(), "gitlab")
assert registry == nil

edge_case_tests.description: Verify POST /auth/callback returns HTTP 400 when the request body is not valid JSON.
preconditions: Echo server with POST /auth/callback handler mounted
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 400; error response body indicating invalid JSON
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
resp := httpPostRaw("/auth/callback", "not-json-{{")
assert resp.statusCode == 400
body := parseJSON(resp.body)
assert body["error"]["code"] == 400

edge_case_tests.description: Verify redirect URI validator rejects https://localhost URIs (HTTPS on localhost is not accepted).
preconditions: Redirect URI validator function
edge_case_tests.expected: Validator returns non-nil error; handler would return HTTP 400

edge_case_tests.description: Verify redirect URI validator rejects a non-localhost URI when external_url is configured but scheme or host does not match.
preconditions: external_url configured as "https://api.example.com"
edge_case_tests.expected: Validator returns non-nil error; HTTP 400 from handler

edge_case_tests.description: Verify HTTP 401 is returned without goroutine leak when request context is cancelled while Exchange is in flight.
preconditions: Mock provider Exchange that blocks until context is cancelled
Request context cancelled after 50ms
edge_case_tests.input: POST /auth/callback where context is cancelled mid-Exchange
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 401 or 499 (connection closed); no goroutine leak; Exchange returns promptly after cancellation

edge_case_tests.description: Verify HTTP 502 is returned without retry when identity provider UserInfo API returns HTTP 401 or 403.
preconditions: httptest.Server standing in as GitHub userinfo endpoint returning HTTP 401
edge_case_tests.input: POST /auth/callback where mock provider UserInfo returns error from HTTP 401 upstream
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 502; body {"error":{"code":502,"message":"failed to retrieve user info from provider"}}; no retry
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
mockProvider.UserInfo = func() { return nil, errors.New("401 Unauthorized") }
resp := httpPost("/auth/callback", validBody)
assert resp.statusCode == 502
body := parseJSON(resp.body)
assert body["error"]["message"] == "failed to retrieve user info from provider"

edge_case_tests.description: Verify HTTP 500 is returned and transaction is rolled back when a database error occurs during user upsert.
preconditions: SQLite database where users table is intentionally broken (e.g. dropped column) to cause insert failure
Mock provider returns valid UserInfo
edge_case_tests.input: POST /auth/callback with valid body; DB insert will fail
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 500; no partial rows in users or api_keys; transaction rolled back
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
// Simulate DB failure by dropping a required column before the request
db.Exec("ALTER TABLE users RENAME COLUMN email TO email_old")
resp := httpPost("/auth/callback", validBody)
assert resp.statusCode == 500
body := parseJSON(resp.body)
assert body["error"]["code"] == 500
// Verify no partial data
userCount := db.QueryRow("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users WHERE provider_id=?", testProviderID).count
assert userCount == 0

edge_case_tests.description: Verify new user gets role='user' when admin_config table is empty (no admin_email configured).
preconditions: SQLite database with empty admin_config table
Mock provider returns UserInfo{Email:"user@example.com", ProviderID:"101"}
edge_case_tests.expected: New user created with role='user'; HTTP 200 response
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
db.Exec("DELETE FROM admin_config")
resp := httpPost("/auth/callback", validBody)
assert resp.statusCode == 200
body := parseJSON(resp.body)
assert body["user"]["role"] == "user"

edge_case_tests.description: Verify new user matching admin_email gets role='user' (not admin) when an admin user already exists.
preconditions: SQLite database with admin_config row admin_email='admin@example.com'
Existing user with role='admin' already in users table
Mock provider returns UserInfo{Email:"admin@example.com", ProviderID:"202"}
edge_case_tests.input: POST /auth/callback for user whose email matches admin_email but admin already exists
edge_case_tests.expected: New user created with role='user'; HTTP 200 response
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
// existing admin already in DB
resp := httpPost("/auth/callback", validBodyForAdminEmail)
assert resp.statusCode == 200
body := parseJSON(resp.body)
assert body["user"]["role"] == "user"

edge_case_tests.description: Verify key generation function returns an error (causing HTTP 500 and transaction rollback) when crypto/rand fails.
preconditions: Key generation function injectable with a mock rand reader that returns an error
edge_case_tests.input: generateAPIKey() called with mock rand that returns error
edge_case_tests.expected: Returns (nil, non-nil error); no key inserted in api_keys; HTTP 500 from handler
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
mockRand := readerThatReturnsError()
keyID, secret, err := generateAPIKey(mockRand)
assert keyID == ""
assert secret == ""
assert err != nil

edge_case_tests.description: Verify key revocation is a no-op and processing continues normally when user has no active keys (first login).
preconditions: SQLite database with no api_keys rows for the user
Mock provider returns valid UserInfo for a new user
edge_case_tests.input: POST /auth/callback for a new user (first login, no existing keys)
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 200; new api_key inserted in api_keys; no error from revocation step
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
resp := httpPost("/auth/callback", validBody)
assert resp.statusCode == 200
body := parseJSON(resp.body)
assert body["api_key"]["key"] != ""
newKey := db.QueryRow("SELECT * FROM api_keys WHERE revoked_at IS NULL")
assert newKey != nil

smoke_tests.description: Full OAuth callback flow for a new user: valid POST /auth/callback results in HTTP 200 with user and api_key, user created in DB, api_key inserted with correct hash.
mockable: GitHub userinfo endpoint (httptest.Server returning login/email/id JSON)
expected_effects: HTTP 200 response with Content-Type: application/json
Response body contains user.id (UUID), user.username, user.email, user.status='active', user.role, user.provider='github'
Response body contains api_key.key matching regex ^ak_[a-zA-Z0-9]{8}_[a-zA-Z0-9]{32}$
Response body api_key.expires_at is RFC 3339 timestamp approximately 90 days from now
SQLite users table contains new row with matching provider='github' and provider_id
SQLite api_keys table contains new row with secret_hash == SHA256(plaintext_secret) and revoked_at IS NULL

smoke_tests.description: Returning user re-login: existing user's username and email are updated, previous API key is revoked, new API key is issued.
smoke_tests.trigger: POST {mount_point}/auth/callback for a user whose (provider, provider_id) already exists in the users table with status='active'
mockable: GitHub userinfo endpoint (httptest.Server returning updated username and email)
expected_effects: HTTP 200 response
users row updated with new username and email; id, role, status, created_at unchanged
Previous api_keys row has revoked_at set to non-null
New api_keys row inserted with revoked_at IS NULL
Response api_key.key is a new key different from the previous one

smoke_tests.description: GET /auth/providers returns provider list with names and authorize URLs, Cache-Control header set, no secrets exposed.
expected_effects: HTTP 200 response
Response header Cache-Control: public, max-age=300
Response body is JSON array with one entry: {"name":"github","authorize_url":"..."}
authorize_url contains client_id and scope=user:email query params
Response body does not contain client_secret, token_url, or userinfo_url

smoke_tests.description: Blocked user OAuth callback is rejected with HTTP 403, user status unchanged, no key created.
smoke_tests.trigger: POST {mount_point}/auth/callback with valid credentials for a user with status='blocked' in the users table
expected_effects: HTTP 403 response
Response body {"error":{"code":403,"message":"user is blocked"}}
users row status remains 'blocked'
api_keys table unchanged (no new row, no revocation)

smoke_tests.description: Server startup with valid TOML config registers github provider in registry and makes both OAuth endpoints reachable.
smoke_tests.trigger: Server bootstrap with config containing one [[oauth.providers]] entry for github; call RegisterOAuthHandlers
expected_effects: Registry.Get("github") returns non-nil GitHub provider
Registry.List() returns ["github"]
GET /auth/providers returns HTTP 200 with [{"name":"github","authorize_url":"..."}]
POST /auth/callback returns HTTP 400 (validation error) not 404
No startup error returned
